steps.title: Calculate the cost per tablet including tax
steps.content:
Each tablet costs \$164. With 6% tax applied to the total order, the effective cost per tablet is:
$$164 \times 1.06 = 173.84$$

steps.title: Find the maximum number of tablets the budget allows
steps.content:
Set up the constraint: if the school orders $n$ tablets, the total cost is $173.84n$, and this must not exceed \$44,800:
$$173.84n \leq 44,800$$
$$n \leq \frac{44,800}{173.84} \approx 257.7$$
Since the school can only order whole tablets, the maximum number is 257.
